signals.headline: New Iran strikes on Gulf as US attacks escalate: What we know
signals.summary: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has targeted US military facilities in Bahrain, claimed it has destroyed radar systems in Oman and hit Jordan and Kuwait in its latest round of overnight retaliatory strikes against the United States.
keyTakeaways: Bahrain: The IRGC also said it launched missile and drone attacks targeting “installations and infrastructure of the aggressive US army” in Juffair, Bahrain.
It said any explosions heard were the result of air defence systems intercepting the attacks and urged the public to follow safety and security instructions.

The US military’s Central Command (CENTCOM) earlier said it hit “dozens of targets at multiple locations with precision munitions to degrade Iran’s ability to continue attacking international shipping flowing through the Strait of Hormuz”.
signals.whyItMatters: Tehran’s attacks on Monday came as a response to Washington’s escalating strikes as prospects of peace between the two countries recede.

ja.headline: イランが湾岸地域への攻撃を拡大、米軍施設も標的に
ja.summary: イラン革命防衛隊（IRGC）は、バーレーンの米軍施設を攻撃したと発表。オマーンのレーダーシステムを破壊し、ヨルダンとクウェートにも攻撃を加えたと主張した。一連の報復攻撃は夜間にかけて行われた。
keyTakeaways: バーレーンのジュファイルにある米軍の施設・インフラに対し、ミサイルおよびドローンによる攻撃を実施したとIRGCは述べた。
現地で聞こえた爆発音は迎撃システムによるものだとし、市民に安全指示への従順を呼びかけた。
米中央軍（CENTCOM）はこれに先立ち、ホルムズ海峡の航行を脅かすイランの能力を低下させるため、複数拠点の数十の目標を精密兵器で攻撃したと発表していた。
ja.whyItMatters: イランの攻撃は米国の軍事行動の激化に対する報復であり、両国間の和平の見通しはさらに遠のいている。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Iran launched more strikes overnight?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Iran's Revolutionary Guard says it targeted US military facilities in Bahrain, and also hit sites in Oman, Jordan, and Kuwait.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What exactly did they hit in Bahrain?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They claim they launched missiles and drones at US military installations in an area called Juffair. Bahrain's government said explosions people heard were from air defense systems intercepting the attacks.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And this is all in response to US strikes?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. The US military had just hit dozens of Iranian targets, saying they wanted to stop Iran from attacking ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So both sides are escalating?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That's the concern. These latest Iranian strikes came as a direct response to Washington's attacks, and prospects for peace between the two countries are fading.

signals.headline: How former Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani built Qatar’s economy
signals.summary: Qatar’s Father Emir Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Al Thani has died at the age of 74.
keyTakeaways: During his 18-year rule, Sheikh Hamad reshaped the energy-rich country’s domestic and global footprint.
When he assumed power in 1995, Qatar’s economy was limited in size and relied mainly on oil, while the vast gas wealth of the North Field site was still in the early stages of development.

In less than two decades, Qatar became the world’s largest exporter of liquefied natural gas (LNG), the owner of one of the largest sovereign wealth funds and one of the countries with the highest per capita incomes.
signals.whyItMatters: Here, we take a look at Sheikh Hamad’s economic legacy that helped transform Qatar from a small Gulf economy to a major and influential player in global energy and investment markets.

ja.headline: カタールを世界的エネルギー大国へ導いた前首長の遺産
ja.summary: カタールの前首長、ハマド・ビン・ハリーファ・アール・サーニー氏が74歳で死去した。18年にわたる統治のなかで、同氏はこの小さな湾岸国を世界の舞台へと押し上げた。
keyTakeaways: 1995年の就任当時、カタール経済は石油依存の小規模なものにとどまり、北部ガス田の開発もまだ緒についたばかりだった。
その後20年足らずで、カタールは液化天然ガスの世界最大輸出国へと成長した。
政府系ファンドの規模と国民一人あたりの所得は、世界有数の水準に達した。
ja.whyItMatters: ハマド前首長の経済的遺産を振り返ることは、カタールがいかにして湾岸の小国からエネルギーと投資の世界的な中心地へと変貌したかを理解することでもある。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw that Qatar's former leader passed away. Sheikh Hamad, I think?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani. He died at seventy-four. He ruled Qatar for eighteen years.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What did he do during that time?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: He really transformed the country's economy. When he came to power in 1995, Qatar was small and relied mainly on oil.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And he changed that?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: He did. Qatar had this huge natural gas field that wasn't fully developed yet. Under his leadership, the country became the world's largest exporter of liquefied natural gas.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's a big shift.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It was. In less than twenty years, Qatar also built one of the largest sovereign wealth funds and became one of the highest per capita income countries in the world.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So he turned Qatar into a major energy player.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. From a small Gulf economy to a major force in global energy and investment markets.

signals.headline: Ann Widdecombe death: counter-terrorism police take over investigation
signals.summary: The force initially said counter-terrorism partners had reviewed the case and had ruled out a terrorist motive.
keyTakeaways: A statement from CTP said “new information and evidence has come to light during what has been a dynamic and complex investigation and as a result, counter-terrorism policing south east is now leading the investigation”.
The man in custody has since been rearrested on suspicion of commission, preparation or instigation of acts of terrorism.

The head of national counter-terrorism policing, Laurence Taylor, said: “Building on the progress made by our colleagues in Devon and Cornwall police, we now have new information and evidence that means counter-terrorism policing is now leading the investigation.
signals.whyItMatters: A 28-year-old white British man from Rotherham, South Yorkshire, was arrested on suspicion of murder on Saturday.

ja.headline: ウィデコム氏死亡事件、対テロ警察が捜査を引き継ぐ
ja.summary: 当初、地元警察は対テロ部門が事件を検討した結果、テロの動機は否定されたと発表していた。しかしその後、新たな情報と証拠が浮上し、対テロ警察南東部が捜査の主導権を握ることになった。
keyTakeaways: 対テロ警察南東部は声明で、「捜査が進む中で新たな情報と証拠が明らかになった」として、正式に捜査を引き継いだと発表した。
身柄を拘束されていた男は、テロ行為の実行・準備・扇動の疑いで再逮捕された。
国家対テロ警察責任者のローレンス・テイラー氏は、デボン・コーンウォール警察の捜査を引き継ぐ形で、新たな情報と証拠に基づき対テロ部門が主導すると述べた。
ja.whyItMatters: 事件をめぐっては、サウスヨークシャー州ロザラム出身の28歳の白人英国人男性が、土曜日に殺人の疑いで逮捕されている。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw counter-terrorism police have taken over the Ann Widdecombe case. What changed?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Initially, they reviewed it and ruled out a terrorist motive. But now they say new information and evidence came to light during the investigation.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So they've changed their assessment?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Counter-terrorism policing south east is now leading what they're calling a dynamic and complex investigation.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What about the person in custody?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A 28-year-old man from Rotherham was arrested on suspicion of murder on Saturday. He's since been rearrested on suspicion of terrorism-related offences.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's quite a shift in the investigation.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. The head of national counter-terrorism policing said they're building on the earlier work and following where the new evidence leads.

signals.headline: How Philips Hue got the smart home right
signals.summary: The state of the smart home can be frustrating, because it is just so obvious how things ought to work.
keyTakeaways: Making your home smart shouldn’t require renovating, and the smarts should be mostly invisible.
Until now, maybe no product has come closer to nailing the smart home than Philips Hue.

The Verge ’s David Pierce and Jennifer Pattison Tuohy are joined by smart home journalist and expert Richard Gunther to discuss Hue’s origins, which have an awful lot to do with the rise of the smartphone.
signals.whyItMatters: Shall We Go On Sinning So That Grace May Increase?

ja.headline: スマートホームの理想形——Philips Hueが示したもの
ja.summary: スマートホームの現状には、もどかしさを感じることが多い。どうあるべきかは、誰の目にも明らかなのに、と。
keyTakeaways: 家をスマート化するために、大がかりな工事は必要ない。技術は、できる限り目立たないほうがいい。
これまでのところ、その理想にもっとも近づいた製品がPhilips Hueだといえる。
The VergeのDavid PierceとJennifer Pattison Tuohyが、スマートホーム専門家のRichard Gunterを迎え、Hueの誕生とスマートフォンの台頭との深い関係を語る。
ja.whyItMatters: 恵みを増やすために、罪の中にとどまり続けるべきなのだろうか。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Philips Hue... that's the smart light bulb system, right?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. And apparently it's one of the few smart home products that's actually gotten things right.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What does that mean, getting it right?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Well, the idea is that making your home smart shouldn't require tearing down walls or renovating. The technology should just blend in.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And Hue does that?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: According to this piece, it comes closer than maybe any other product so far. You just screw in a bulb.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How did it get started?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Interestingly, its origins are tied to the rise of smartphones. The Verge brought in a smart home expert to talk about how it all came together.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So smartphones made smart lights possible?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Pretty much. Once everyone had a powerful computer in their pocket, controlling your lights from an app started to make sense.

signals.headline: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
signals.summary: Apple’s self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company’s chips the powerful AI performers they are.
keyTakeaways: Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing.
While Apple’s AI software efforts have lagged behind the rest of the industry, its hardware has been impressive.
It’s also what has allowed Apple to tout its privacy features, since less data is sent to the cloud.
signals.whyItMatters: The M7 Ultra is expected to be the basis for a new server product from Apple as well, with support for up to 1.5TB of RAM.

ja.headline: 幻に終わった自動運転が、Appleのチップを育てた
ja.summary: Appleの自動運転プロジェクトは日の目を見ることなく幕を閉じた。しかしその過程が、同社チップを強力なAI処理の担い手へと鍛え上げた可能性がある。
keyTakeaways: 自動運転の開発初期、Appleはデバイス上での高度なAI処理の必要性に気づいていた
AIソフトウェアでは業界に後れを取る一方、ハードウェアの完成度は際立っている
処理をデバイス内で完結させる設計は、プライバシー保護の訴求にもつながっている
ja.whyItMatters: M7 Ultraは最大1.5TBのメモリに対応し、Appleの新たなサーバー製品の基盤になるとも見られている。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Apple tried to make a self-driving car?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They did, yeah. The program never really got off the ground, but there's an interesting side effect.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What came out of it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Early on, Apple realized they'd need really powerful chips to handle AI processing right on the device. That pushed their chip development forward.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the car project actually helped their other products?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. While their AI software has lagged behind competitors, the hardware itself has been impressive. Those chips are now in their computers and phones.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Does that affect how we use Apple devices?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It does. Because the chips can handle more on the device itself, less of your data needs to be sent to the cloud. That's become a big privacy selling point for Apple.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Interesting how a failed project can still leave something valuable behind.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. And they're now planning a new server product based on that chip technology, with support for up to one and a half terabytes of memory.
